name: Simurgh

visual_attributes.morphology: winged creature in the shape of a bird
distinctive_features: gigantic size capable of carrying an elephant or whale
peacock-like body with the head of a dog or human

domain_specific_visual.iconography: appears in Iranian art and literature
domain_specific_visual.cultural_influence: found in Persian, Georgian, Armenian, and Eastern Roman iconography

domain_specific_functional.mythological_role: possessor of all knowledge and wisdom

relationships.description: The Simurgh raised Zal after he was abandoned by his father.
The Simurgh assisted in the birth of Rudaba's child, Rostam.
The Simurgh has an enmity towards snakes.

contextual_attributes.geographic_range: Persia, Georgia, Armenia, Eastern Roman Empire
contextual_attributes.cultural_significance: symbol of divine glory and wisdom

contrastive_constraints: NOT a purely avian creature without mammalian features
